title: /libpod/images/prune endpoint does not support the dangling filter
body:
/kind bug


**Description**

The `/libpod/images/prune` endpoint does not remove unused images.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Have some unused images

2.  ```curl -X POST -g --no-buffer --unix-socket /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock 'http://localhost/v1.12/libpod/images/prune?filters={"dangling":["true"]}'```

**Describe the results you received:**

```
null
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

Unused images are removed.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Dangling support is not implemented yet in the code, the filters are removed https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/api/handlers/libpod/images.go#L177

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 3.3.1
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

comments.body: I am pretty sure that `image prune` removes all dangling images by default and `--all` includes all other unused images. It actually seems to serve no purpose here. @jwhonce should we remove support for dangling? in the code right now it is deleted if specified.
I've figured out how to replicate `podman image prune -a` with the REST API by calling `/images/prune?all=true` but this was not documented at the time. It now is.

@cdoern We should drag @baude into this. I'm not sure what his intent was, or the code has changed under the handler. The dangling filter is removed in favor ImagePruneOptions.All, then the libpod code restores the dangling filter from ImagePruneOptions.All. Seems like we could just use the original filter.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@jwhonce @cdoern do the right thing here. I am too far removed from it.  We can also discuss in water cooler tomorrow if there is any open questions ...
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@cdoern, do you know the status of this issue?
@vrothberg not sure, I really think this is an example of just some necessary code removal, `--dangling` does not seem to do anything cc/ @jwhonce @baude
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

We have been carrying this for a year, is "danglng" documented somewhere when it comes to prune?
```
$ podman image prune --help
Remove unused images
Description:

  Removes dangling or unused images from local storage.
```
Passing dangling filter makes no sense.
Cockpit-podman has an prune implementation for a while and it does not require the dangling filter option to work so this issue can be closed in my opinion.
